mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate acts as a persistent drag on equity valuations because a high discount rate compresses the present value of future corporate earnings. At the current snapshot of 5.06%, the rate remains elevated relative to recessionary conditions, implying continued negative pressure on the S&P 500 over the next month.
failure_condition: If the rank information coefficient (IC) between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20-day returns remains below -0.03 or if positive returns occur more than 60% of the time when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in housing starts below 1,000,000 units signals a collapse in residential investment and consumer wealth, leading to deteriorating corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The current level of 1,401 is above the threshold but the economy is approaching it.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts fell dramatically in late 2008
failure_condition: If in periods where HOUST dips below 1000 the forward 20-day SPX return is positive more than 50% of the time, or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in payroll employment exceeding 200,000 and real retail sales exceeding 2% indicate a collapse in consumer income and spending. This joint contraction propagates to corporate cash flows, driving S&P 500 forward returns lower over a 20-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM change < -200 and RSAFS MoM change < -2%
failure_condition: If the joint signal produces positive average 20-day returns, or if the precision (positive predictive value) for negative returns is below 60% over a 2-year rolling walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime (UNRATE above 6%), a month-over-month increase in industrial production of more than 1% signals a nascent recovery in manufacturing. This improvement in supply-side conditions boosts investor confidence and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The current snapshot shows UNRATE at 3.7% but the recession pushing it higher.
failure_condition: If INDPRO rises >1% MoM while UNRATE >6% and the forward 20-day return is negative or flat in more than half the occurrences, or if the mean return is not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A high level of retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which supports corporate revenues and earnings. Regardless of other conditions, a high absolute level of sales is a persistent bullish signal for the S&P 500 over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: If the correlation between RSAFS level and forward 5-day return is not positive, or if the mean return in the top tertile of RSAFS is not significantly above zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals contraction in the manufacturing sector. When production falls, corporate cash flows are expected to deteriorate, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The mechanism is triggered when the month-over-month change in INDPRO turns negative.
failure_condition: If the mean 60-day return after a negative INDPRO month is not negative, or if the signal yields a positive return in more than 45% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP is contracting (negative quarterly change) and the federal funds rate remains above 3%, monetary policy is still restrictive during an economic downturn. This mismatch deepens the recession and strongly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The joint condition amplifies the negative impact beyond either factor alone.
failure_condition: If the joint condition is not followed by negative 60-day returns in at least 70% of instances, or if the interaction term in a regression is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a severe recession with unemployment above 7%, a monthly increase in the CPI (above 0.2%) signals that demand is recovering and deflation risk is receding. This bolsters corporate pricing power and investor sentiment, driving the S&P 500 higher over the next 20 trading days. The current recession is pushing unemployment toward that threshold.
failure_condition: If CPI rises >0.2% MoM when UNRATE >7% and forward 20-day return is negative or not significantly positive (p > 0.10).
